EMA REPORTS AT LEAST 100 TREES DOWN...A MOBILE HOME CRUSHED BY A TREE BLOWN DOWN...AND A TIN ROOF BLOWN OFF OF A WORKING BARN ALONG A 2.5 MILE LONG PATH FROM THE DOGWOOD VALLEY AREA TO THE EAST LEE DRIVE AREA. SEVERAL HOMES HAD SHINGLE DAMAGE AND DAMAGE FROM TREES FALLING ALONG THE PATH.
DOORS BLOWN IN AT HORSE ARENA JUST EAST OF INTERSTATE 75 ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF TOWN. CAMPER AWNINGS DAMAGED. BILLBOARD DOWN. POSSIBLE TORNADO. ONE PERSON REPORTS SEEING THE FUNNEL CLOUD.

MINOR DAMAGE ASSOCIATED WITH AN EF-0 TORNADO...WIND ESTIMATED 70 MPH. DAMAGE BEGAN ON TAYLOR ST WEST OF I-75...ONE TREE FELL DAMAGING THE CORNER OF A HOUSE AND ANOTHER HOUSE LOST ROOF SHINGLES. PATH CONTINUED EAST OF I-75 ALONG ARENA RD WHERE TIN ROOFING WAS TORN FROM A ROW OF HORSE STALLS AND A LARGE WOOD BILLBOARD WAS BLOWN OVER ON A CAR. PATH ENDED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF 3RD DISTRICT RD AND BROWNS CHAPEL RD WHERE THE TOP OF A TREE WAS BROKEN OFF. PATH ENE 1 MI LONG...LESS THAN 100 YDS WIDE.

EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE AL STATE LINE ON STATE LINE ROAD THEN CONTINUED ON A NEAR 4 MILE LONG PATH ENDING APPROXIMATELY 2.6 MILES NORTHWEST OF TRION ON CENTER POST ROAD. PATH WIDTH - 300 YARDS WIDE...ESTIMATED MAX WINDS 95-105 MPH. MAJORITY OF THE DAMAGE OCCURRED ALONG THE MID-END PART OF PATH ALONG HEMPHILL ROAD...WHERE 8 HOMES WERE DAMAGED...2 GARAGES...A CARPORT...2 AIRPLANE HANGERS DESTROYED..1 ULTRA LIGHT AND 1 CESSNA PLANE FLIPPED OVER...AND 100S OF LARGE TREES WERE DESTROYED.

A SMALL DAM HAS BEEN BREECHED NEAR POST AND LIBERTY ROADS. WATER WAS FLOWING INTO WOODLAND AREAS AND NOT AFFECTING HOMES. SEVERAL REPORTS OF BASEMENTS FLOODED HAVE BEEN RECIEVED THROUGHOUT THE COUNTY.

POND WAS OVERTOPPED BY HEAVY RAIN AND WATER AT LEAST 6 INCHES DEEP WAS OVERFLOWING ROSE AVENUE. SEVERAL ROADS HAVE BEEN CLOSED IN THE COUNTY DUE TO SLOW DRAINAGE.

FLOOD WATERS FROM THE UPPER SOUTH FORK PEACHTREE CREEK ENTER THE CRAWL SPACES OF APARTMENTS AT LANSBURY VILLAGE DRIVE. AROUND 45 PEOPLE WERE EVACUATED UNTIL THE BUILDINGS ARE INSPECTED FOR STRUCTURE PROBLEMS.

COUNTY EMA REPORTED A WASHOUT OF A SERVICE ROAD AND PARKING LOT IN FRONT OF A CAR WASH AND SONIC RESTAURANT OFF OF U.S. HWY 129. SIGNIFICANT EROSION OF 6 TO 8 FEET DOWN OCCURRED WITH PAVEMENT COLLAPSED AND BUSINESS AWNINGS DAMAGED. A FLOOD SURVEY INDICATED A SINKHOLE HAD BEEN DEVELOPING JUST NORTH OF THE CAR WASH PRIOR TO THE HEAVY RAIN EVENT DUE TO UNDERGROUND EROSION OF A CORRODING STORM DRAIN PIPE. THE EXCESSIVE RUNOFF EXPLOITED THIS WEAK AREA.

A FAST MOVING TORNADO RATED EF-1 TOUCHED DOWN IN THE SOUTH PART OF RISING FAWN AT 522 AM EDT. THE TORNADO CONTINUED ABOUT 2.5 MILES LIFTING NEAR HOLDER LOOP ROAD ABOUT 2 MILES EAST OF RISING FAWN AT 525 AM EDT. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE 100 MPH. THE MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 150 YDS. 100-200 TREES WERE DOWNED AND 30 HOMES RECEIVED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. 2 BARNS WERE DESTROYED AND A MOBILE HOME HAD MOST OF ITS ROOF RIPPED OFF.
